boot_phases.title: Need-state initialization
boot_phases.description: Validate Tier 1 physiological inputs and seed the boot handoff with HOLD defaults.

boot_phases.title: Safety scan
boot_phases.description: Validate Tier 2 safety inputs and the minimum firmware safety envelope.

boot_phases.title: Identity calibration
boot_phases.description: Bind operator identity, temporal frame, and calibration context into the handoff record.

boot_phases.title: Governance check
boot_phases.description: Confirm policy, provenance, and execution-authority constraints before loading the filesystem target.

boot_phases.title: Internal probe
boot_phases.description: Probe NSIGII firmware health, memory map integrity, and runtime compatibility signals.

boot_phases.title: Integrity verification
boot_phases.description: Verify discriminant, artifact manifest, and kernel entry constraints before handoff.

filesystem_target.lookup_policy: first FAT12 root directory match, then fallback_artifact

kernel_handoff_contract.contract_rule: The bootloader must fully populate MMUKO_BOOT_HANDOFF before attempting kernel transfer.
kernel_handoff_contract.kernel_rule: The kernel must validate magic, revision, outcome PASS, completed_phases == 6, and handoff_checksum before executing runtime initialization.

nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_1: Implement all six canonical phases using the exact phase identifiers listed above.
nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_2: Default the handoff outcome to HOLD until integrity verification promotes it to PASS.
nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_3: Abort with ALERT when Tier 1 or Tier 2 requirements resolve to NO.
nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_4: Persist the filesystem target, kernel artifact path, and manifest path in the handoff contract.
nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_5: Expose phase completion, outcome codes, and validation flags to native runtime consumers.
nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_6: Reject handoff if the kernel artifact or manifest cannot be resolved from the FAT12 target.
nsigii_firmware_requirements.fw_7: Treat NSIGII firmware compatibility as a precondition for the kernel handoff contract.
